chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q1_analysis: The paper explicitly states: "VampOS's code is available at https://zenodo.org/records/10784506." This provides a working link to the artifacts.
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q2_analysis: The evaluation was prototyped on Unikraft 0.8.0 and QEMU 6.1.50, using real applications (SQLite, Nginx, Redis, Echo) running on physical hardware (PowerEdge R740). This constitutes a highly realistic simulation/testbed environment.
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q3_analysis: Section II-B provides a dedicated section titled 'Fault Model', explicitly defining the scope: VampOS recovers from fail-stop faults of the unikernel components, mentioning software aging (memory leaks) and non-deterministic hardware faults. It also clearly states what it does not cover (deterministic bugs, permanent hardware failures).
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q4_analysis: The paper provides rigorous, data-driven analysis of trade-offs by measuring runtime overheads (e.g., 1.46x penalty in Fig. 7) and memory space overheads (Table III), and comparing performance across different optimization configurations (VampOS-Noop vs VampOS-DaS vs VampOS-FSm, etc.).

major_weakness: The paper is exceptionally strong across all metrics; the only minor weakness noted is that log shrinking does not eliminate all potential long-term aging issues if components lack canceling functions.
